claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about, and what are their ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?**

This is important because for children aged 16 and 17, they generally need to be in full-time education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent) or approved training to qualify for Child Benefit. This doesn't include university or other advanced education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in full-time education - he's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, do you provide for Alex's needs, such as food, clothing, and general care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I provide for all his needs like food, clothing, and general care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

This is important because only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child at any given time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 17 years old and in full-time non-advanced education (studying A-levels)
✓ Alex lives with you
✓ You are responsible for Alex and provide for his needs
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. 

**Important things to note:**
- Child Benefit can continue until Alex turns 20, as long as he remains in approved full-time education or training
- The benefit will stop on 31 August (the end of the school year) after Alex turns 19, or when he leaves education/training, whichever comes first
- If your individual income is over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though you can still claim

If you'd like to proceed with your claim, you can apply online through the government website or by post.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and in full-time non-advanced education (studying A-levels at sixth form college). Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for children under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Alex lives with you, you are responsible for him (providing for his needs such as food, clothing and care), and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for him. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 17), who lives with them. Alex is in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels), which satisfies the requirement for 16-17 year olds. The claimant provides for Alex's needs and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

Alex is in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels), which satisfies the requirement for 16-17 year olds. The claimant provides for Alex's needs and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
